emails.subject: Re: Coastal Ctr# 96008903 Meter 0984179 Sit#135714
emails.body: Re: Coastal Ctr# 96008903 Meter 0984179 Sit#135714 I checked the meter for September, there are measured volumes of 36,085 Dth for the month. The meter had a purchase from MJG at the beginning of the month. It looks like it was zeroed after the fact. We are going to need to pay someone for this production....I think we may need to extend the deal until the point at which the meter was actually shut-in. I do not agree with allocating this gas to Strangers, it is not a true line gain/loss, or a direct result of any pipe/well work. Please advise so that Anita and I can clean this up as promptly as possible. Mary x 35251

emails.subject: Re: Coastal Ctr# 96008903 Meter 0984179 Sit#135714
emails.body: Having discussed the matter with Brian Riley, HPL has no intention of contacting Coastal to negotiate any type of deal affecting the gas received in September at at Meter 4179. It was Brian's opinion that the gas should be considered Strangers gas since HPL has no contract or price. It is suggested that Nick Moshou contact his counterpart at Coastal, relaying this information and suggesting Coastal's trader contract Brian Riley. Donald Mary Poorman@ENRON 10/27/2000 10:05 AM
